extracted.brandBackground: Kumon operates after-school learning centers providing math and reading programs using the Kumon Method.

leadership.name: Yusuke Nakamura
leadership.role: Chief Executive Officer and Chief Operating Officer
leadership.background: Has been with the Kumon organization since 1997 and previously served as President of Kumon Philippines.
leadership.whyItMatters: Provides deep international and operational experience within the Kumon system.

leadership.name: Akiko Kaneshiro
leadership.role: Executive Senior Vice President and Chief Support Officer
leadership.background: Appointed in July 2025, previously serving as Vice President of Overseas Strategy Department for KIE.
leadership.whyItMatters: Brings strategic alignment with the global parent company.

leadership.name: Joseph Nativo
leadership.role: Executive Senior Vice President, Chief Administrative Officer and Chief Financial Officer
leadership.background: Has served as CFO since August 1999 and has been with Kumon since 1992.
leadership.whyItMatters: Ensures long-term financial continuity and administrative oversight.

leadership.name: Nadia Monaco
leadership.role: Senior Vice President and Director of Field Operations / Center Network Development
leadership.background: Appointed in 2025/2026, previously serving as Vice President of Customer Experience.
leadership.whyItMatters: Oversees the direct support and expansion of the franchise network.

item19.notes: The franchisor does not make any representations about a franchisee's future financial performance.

lineItems.notes: Paid at signing of Training Agreement; applied to franchise fee upon completion.
Fee is $2,000 to $3,000, less the $1,000 deposit if first center.
Instruction answer books, achievement tests, placement tests, and promotional material.
Required minimum spend on pre-opening marketing activities.
Estimated cost for architectural design services.
Estimated cost for build-out, net of potential franchisor carpet/paint reimbursement.
Estimated rent for the first three months.
Net of initial furniture/fixtures and exterior sign provided/reimbursed by Kumon.
Estimated annual premium for required liability insurance.
Purchase and installation of the system.
Purchase of approximately 378 books for levels 7A-L.
Estimated payroll for assistants for the first 3 months.
Sexual abuse and molestation prevention training.
Estimated working capital for the first 3 months of operation.

hiddenCosts.name: Monthly Royalty During TLP
hiddenCosts.description: $42.75 per full-payment student and $21.38 per partially exempt/prorated student per subject.

hiddenCosts.name: Monthly Royalty After TLP
hiddenCosts.description: $38 per full-paying student and $19 per partially exempt/prorated student per subject.

hiddenCosts.name: Late or Inaccurate Report Fee
hiddenCosts.description: $200 for the first month, $500 for the second, and $1,000 for the third month and thereafter.

hiddenCosts.name: Relocation Fee
hiddenCosts.description: $2,000 fee if fewer than 80% of enrolled students transfer to the new location.

hiddenCosts.name: Lead Management System Monthly Expense
hiddenCosts.description: Approximately $50 per month plus applicable taxes for use of the system.

operationalRisks.title: Out-of-State Dispute Resolution
operationalRisks.description: The franchise agreement requires you to resolve disputes by mediation, arbitration, and/or litigation only in New Jersey.

operationalRisks.title: Royalty Rate Changes
operationalRisks.description: The franchise agreement permits the franchisor to change royalty rates or due dates on at least one year's notice.

operationalRisks.title: Non-Exclusive Territory
operationalRisks.description: You will not receive an exclusive territory and may face competition from other franchisees or company-owned outlets.

financialCondition.parentName: Kumon Institute of Education Co., Ltd.

extracted.conceptRationale: Kumon is a supplemental after-school learning program focusing on math and reading for children.
extracted.staffingRationale: Centers require the franchisee to act as the full-time instructor and hire assistants as student enrollment increases.

riskReasons: No Item 19 financial performance representation — earnings are undisclosed, which is itself a caution.
A high-severity operational tripwire is disclosed — see the tripwires section.
Franchisee unit economics could not be scored — no usable Item 19 (company/affiliate-owned only, or no FPR). Treat this as UNVERIFIED, not low-risk.
notes: Royalty % not found; variable costs may be understated.
No Item 19 middle-cohort or network-average revenue found; economics are indeterminate.

underwriting.assessment: $250,000 liquid covers the $167,705 mid-point build-out without financing, though that leaves little working-capital cushion.

insights.conceptRationale: Kumon is a supplemental after-school learning program focusing on math and reading for children.

benchmark.label: Education / childcare
benchmark.dominantRisk: Regulated staff ratios and enrollment/utilization. Labor dominates and ratios cap how lean you can run.
considerations: Labor is the dominant line at ~40–55% and is floored by regulated student/staff ratios.
Validate enrollment ramp and capacity-utilization curves with Item 20 operators.
Confirm licensing, facility, and compliance costs specific to your state — not in the FDD.

crossCheck.message: No operating-EBITDA margin is disclosed in Item 19. The 10–20% band below is an industry estimate for this concept — validate it against real franchisee P&Ls (Item 20) before relying on any margin.

assumptions.detail: Classified as “Education / childcare” from the FDD (Item 1 / brand description).

insights.consultCtaPitch: These are category benchmarks applied to the disclosed top line — your unit's real costs will differ, and that difference is the conversation. Want them pressure-tested against actual franchisee P&Ls for your territory?
insights.asOf: 2026 — general industry benchmarks, refine against real franchisee P&Ls
insights.disclaimer: Industry benchmark ranges for your own budgeting and Item 20 questions — NOT a projection of this franchise's results, and not investment advice.

financialCondition.headline: The franchisor's statements don't show the distress signals we screen for — positive net worth and a clean audit. Still worth a quick confirmation against current figures.
body: Why it matters to you: field support, training, technology, and marketing come out of the franchisor's budget. If outside funding slows, those are the first things to get cut. There is no parent guarantee of performance in this FDD, so Kumon Institute of Education Co., Ltd. is not contractually obligated to keep funding the franchisor or backing its commitments to you.

Verify before you commit: ask for the most recent interim (unaudited) financials and the current-year funding plan; ask whether the parent will sign a guarantee of performance; and cross-check Item 20 — unit closures, transfers, and terminations are the operational tell that corroborates financial stress.
aggravators: no parent guarantee backing the franchisor's obligations to you
mitigants: the auditor issued a clean (unmodified) opinion and did not raise going-concern doubt
financialCondition.evidenceNote: Derived from the Item 21 / Exhibit F audited financials. Figures are extracted; all ratios and grading are computed. Confirm against the source statements and a CPA before relying on them.
